title: selinux: remove explicit range transition when starting conmon
body:
Currently, podman enforces a hard-coded transition to s0 when starting up conmon. Instead, we should be using a `range_transition` in the policy to implement this behavior.

This is a companion PR to https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/166

comments.body:
Please fix the lint issues.

You either need to add a test, perhaps running a container in background and checking the label on conmon.
Or add [NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
Ok now we need an updated container-selinux.  I will cut a release.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

The patch itself is complete, but I think it may not apply cleanly onto main since there have been some conflicting changes. Should I rebase this?

Just waiting for this to be merged unless there's some other changes you want.
Yes rebase, and I can test out.
Done. Please review and test. Thanks!
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

After applying this patch on Fedora 36 with container-selinux applied I see this for rootless containers.

With this patch:
$ ./bin/podman run -d alpine sleep 100
d56c00c2ea872d2c4fbfdb6987318d00eadb2f64cc931aff67cdf981b5e0417b
$ ps -eZ | grep conmon
unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 621904 ? 00:00:00 conmon
$ ps -eZ | grep sleep
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c774,c864 621909 ? 00:00:00 sleep

Without this patch
$ /bin/podman run -d alpine sleep 100
9945875772d23f4f3c0d1b02c5025b032e08a4e37761735e84143738d7fd3a5f
$ ps -eZ | grep sleep
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c774,c864 621909 ? 00:00:00 sleep
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c236,c343 622025 ? 00:00:00 sleep
$ ps -eZ | grep conmon
unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 621904 ? 00:00:00 conmon
unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 622022 ? 00:00:00 conmon

Conmon should be running as container_runtime_t:s0 correct in both cases?

> Conmon should be running as container_runtime_t:s0 correct in both cases?

Yes that's correct. I am most surprised by the fact that in your case the patched version is not even running as `container_runtime_t`. In refpolicy we have podman transition to a private type for conmon and that is working in my tests.

Is there any reason why podman running conmon would transition back to `unconfined_t` for some reason, or did some other behavior change where conmon now inherits the type of the user?

I should also point out that in refpolicy unpriv users cannot normally transition to the system role, and so we must manually specify `--security-opt label=user:unconfined_u --security-opt label=role:unconfined_r`. Does that change the behavior in this case?

Ok the issue for me was because I did not label ./bin/podman as container_runtime_exec_t, so unconfined_t@container_runtime_exec_t->container_runtime_t
It was labeled user_home_t
unconfined_t@user_home_t->unconfined_t

$ chcon -t container_runtime_exec_t bin/podman
$ bin/podman run -d alpine sleep 100
f155570234072336155e8e6080c494bb48ae02fb0dad6143b1ddf5240604c901
$ ps -eZ | grep conmon
unconfined_u:system_r:container_runtime_t:s0 71272 ? 00:00:00 conmon
$ ps -eZ | grep sleep
system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c572,c621 71275 ? 00:00:00 sleep

LGTM
